pages.title: Bedroom Lamps That Make Your Room Feel Like a Luxury Hotel
pages.description: The best bedroom lamps for layered, glam lighting. Bedside tables, reading corners, dresser tops — all covered.
pages.intro: Good bedroom lighting isn't one lamp. It's three sources — and none of them are overhead.
pages.tip: For bedside lamps, the shade bottom should sit at eye level when you're sitting up reading — about 20–22 inches above the nightstand surface.

faqs.question: What size lamp do I need for a bedside table?
faqs.answer: The lamp height should put the bottom of the shade at eye level when you're sitting up in bed — roughly 20 to 22 inches above the nightstand surface. For a standard 28-inch nightstand, that means a total lamp height of about 24 to 26 inches. The shade width should be no wider than two thirds of the nightstand's surface.

faqs.question: Should bedside lamps match?
faqs.answer: Matching lamps are the safer choice and look intentional in almost any style. Mismatched lamps can work in boho or eclectic rooms, but both need to sit at the same height so the light level reads as balanced. The safest mismatch: same base finish, different shade shape.

faqs.question: How many lumens do I need for a bedroom lamp?
faqs.answer: Bedside reading lamps need 400 to 500 lumens. Ambient mood lamps work best at 200 to 300 lumens. Avoid anything over 600 lumens on a nightstand — it reads clinical and harsh. Warm white at 2700K to 3000K makes any bedroom feel more relaxed regardless of brightness level.

faqs.question: What lamp style works best in a glam bedroom?
faqs.answer: Crystal or glass bases with gold hardware are the most versatile choice for glam. They catch light without competing with bedding or art. Drum shades in ivory or blush keep the look soft. Avoid chrome finishes — they read cold next to warm textiles. Brushed or satin gold is the sweet spot.

pages.body:
The lamp on your nightstand is the last thing you see at night and the first light you reach for in the morning. Get it wrong and your bedroom feels like a waiting room. The base looks cheap, the shade tilts, and no amount of good bedding fixes it.

Get it right and the room transforms. A crystal base catches the glow. A warm-white bulb turns ordinary walls into something worth photographing. Layered light — bedside lamp, dresser lamp, maybe a floor lamp in the corner — makes a bedroom feel intentional rather than assembled.

This guide covers every lamp type that works in a bedroom, sized and styled correctly, from budget picks to statement pieces worth the investment.

pages.title: Living Room Mirrors That Add Space, Light, and a Lot More Style
pages.description: The best mirrors for living rooms — from sunburst statement pieces to full-length floor mirrors. Sized and styled for your wall.
pages.intro: A well-placed living room mirror doubles your light, anchors your biggest wall, and makes the room feel twice as finished.
pages.tip: Hang a mirror so its centre sits at eye level — about 57 to 60 inches from the floor. Going higher makes ceilings feel low; going lower makes the room feel unresolved.

faqs.question: Where should a mirror go in a living room?
faqs.answer: Above the sofa is the most common placement and works in almost every layout. The mirror should be roughly two thirds the width of the sofa below it. Above a fireplace mantel is the second most popular spot — here the mirror can be larger, even floor-to-ceiling. Avoid placing mirrors directly opposite windows if there is harsh midday glare.

faqs.question: What size mirror works above a sofa?
faqs.answer: Aim for a mirror that is 50 to 75 percent of the sofa's width. For a standard 84-inch sofa, that means a mirror between 42 and 63 inches wide. Going narrower looks like an afterthought. Going wider than the sofa can work with a very large wall but requires careful ceiling height.

faqs.question: Do living room mirrors have to match other metal finishes in the room?
faqs.answer: They don't have to match exactly, but they should be in the same family. Gold and brass work together. Matte black and gunmetal work together. Mixing gold and chrome in the same room creates visual noise. If you have mixed metals already, choose a mirror finish that leans toward the dominant metal in your other fixtures.

pages.body:
A living room without a mirror is a room that isn't using its walls properly. The right mirror does three things at once: it reflects natural light deeper into the room, it fills a wall that needs an anchor, and it adds a layer of visual interest that art alone rarely achieves.

For glam interiors, the mirror is often the centrepiece. A sunburst in antique gold above the sofa. A full-length arch mirror leaning against the feature wall. An oversized round mirror with a bevelled edge catching every light source in the room.

This page covers the mirrors that work in living rooms specifically — the right sizes, the right hanging heights, and the styles that pull the whole room together.

pages.title: Luxury Glam Lamps — Gold, Crystal, and Statement Lighting
pages.description: Luxury glam lamps with gold bases, crystal details, and velvet shades. Curated picks for living rooms, bedrooms, and entryways.
pages.intro: Luxury glam lighting isn't about brightness. It's about the glow — warm, golden, reflected off every surface in the room.
pages.tip: Satin gold photographs warmer and reads more luxurious in person than polished gold. If you're choosing between finishes, satin is the safer pick for any room that gets natural light.

faqs.question: What makes a lamp 'luxury glam' style?
faqs.answer: The defining elements are metallic finishes (gold, brass, or rose gold), reflective materials (crystal, glass, or mirrored bases), and rich fabric shades (velvet, silk, or pleated linen in ivory or blush). Shape matters too — curved silhouettes, tapered shades, and ornate base details read as glam. Industrial shapes, flat shades, and matte black finishes do not.

faqs.question: Do luxury glam lamps have to be expensive?
faqs.answer: No. The glam look is driven by finish and shape more than price. A $75 crystal table lamp with a gold base reads as luxury when styled well. The difference at higher price points is build quality — the weight of the base, the consistency of the plating, the fabric quality of the shade. For rented spaces or guest rooms, the budget version is perfectly fine.

faqs.question: Can glam lamps work in a modern or minimalist room?
faqs.answer: Yes, one glam lamp in a minimalist room works as an intentional contrast piece. The key is restraint — one statement lamp against clean lines reads as curated. Three glam lamps in the same minimalist room starts to look like a mismatch. Pair a crystal base with a simple linen shade to soften the drama.

pages.body:
Luxury glam lighting starts with gold. Not the brassy gold of another era — the warm, brushed, editorial gold that appears in every hotel suite worth photographing. Pair it with crystal, marble, or mirrored glass and the lamp stops being a light source and becomes a feature.

The right glam lamp makes everything around it look more considered. It elevates cheap bedding. It makes a plain wall feel styled. It does more visual work per dollar than almost any other single decor purchase in a room.

This page covers the best luxury glam lamps across every room and price tier — from $49 bedside picks to statement floor lamps that anchor a living room completely.
